title: The One with the Tiny T-Shirt

jokes.text: Say, Rachel... I was wondering if you'd like to go to a movie with me sometime... as my lover.
jokes.setup: Gunther practicing asking Rachel out
jokes.punchline: The overly intimate 'as my lover' phrasing
jokes.explanation: Classic Gunther awkwardness - the formal phrasing paired with 'lover' is perfectly cringe and character-specific

jokes.text: Mm. Too out there. Maybe you'd just like to get something to eat with me sometime... as my lover.
jokes.setup: Gunther recognizing the movie line was too bold
jokes.punchline: Still ending with the same inappropriate 'as my lover'
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation of the same joke - he thinks the problem was the activity, not the phrasing

jokes.text: [CRASH IN KITCHEN]
jokes.setup: Mark just asked Rachel out in front of Gunther
jokes.punchline: Gunther's shocked reaction causes him to drop a cup
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical punctuation to Gunther's emotional state - economical visual gag

jokes.text: I dropped a cup.
jokes.setup: Obviously loud crash from kitchen
jokes.punchline: Gunther's matter-of-fact explanation of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan - the understatement makes his embarrassment funnier

jokes.text: It's not gonna be able to do any of those things... but it'll understand what you're saying.
jokes.setup: Mark explaining voice recognition will let you command your computer
jokes.punchline: The technology will understand but can't actually do anything useful
jokes.explanation: Smart tech humor that feels ahead of its time - good observational comedy about the gap between promise and reality

jokes.text: We were out of Doritos.
jokes.setup: Monica says she gave them keys for emergencies
jokes.punchline: Running out of snacks qualifies as an emergency to them
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - shows their priorities and complete lack of boundaries in one line

jokes.text: Mr. Millionaire. New, from Snooty Playthings. Third wife sold separately.
jokes.setup: Joey calling Pete 'Mr. Millionaire'
jokes.punchline: Chandler turns it into a fake toy commercial with dark implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant improvised commercial parody - 'Snooty Playthings' and 'sold separately' are perfect touches that make this highly quotable

jokes.text: Life-sized Imperial Storm Troopers from Sharper Image? Two.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining Pete has everything
jokes.punchline: The specific absurd luxury item, and that he has multiples
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of wealth absurdity - Sharper Image reference dates it but the excess is funny

jokes.text: Wow. Can Joey and I put them on and fight?
jokes.setup: Hearing about expensive Star Wars collectibles
jokes.punchline: Immediately wanting to play with them like toys
jokes.explanation: Captures their childlike enthusiasm - good character beat showing they'd treat expensive collectibles as playthings

jokes.text: Oh, Mommy. Oh, Daddy. I am a big old baddy.
jokes.setup: Joey presumably putting on or mimicking Storm Trooper gear
jokes.punchline: His childish villain voice and phrasing
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey silliness - 'big old baddy' is perfectly childish phrasing for his character

jokes.text: Either that or Gloria Estefan was right. Eventually, the rhythm is going to get you.
jokes.setup: Seeing Joey dancing, guessing he got the part
jokes.punchline: Reference to Gloria Estefan song as alternative explanation
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that works as alternative explanation - typical Chandler wit

jokes.text: Are you one of the retarded cousins?
jokes.setup: Joey asking if Kate is playing Adrianne
jokes.punchline: Kate's blunt assumption about Joey's casting
jokes.explanation: Establishes Kate's pretentious attitude and Joey's typical casting - dated language affects impact

jokes.text: She's so brilliantly incisive when it comes to deconstructing the psyche of the middle class... Oh, forget about it. She rocks.
jokes.setup: Kate using pretentious theater language about the playwright
jokes.punchline: Joey's simple 'She rocks' translation
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between their vocabularies - Joey's simplification is endearing and honest

jokes.text: Dr. Drake Ramoray? Days of our Lives? Voted most datable neurosurgeon by Teen Beat?
jokes.setup: Kate asking where she knows Joey from
jokes.punchline: Joey's increasingly specific and ridiculous credentials
jokes.explanation: 'Most datable neurosurgeon by Teen Beat' is perfectly absurd - captures Joey's pride in soap fame

jokes.text: So you're a soap actor. Well, this must be exciting for you, being in a real play.
jokes.setup: Kate recognizing Joey from soap opera
jokes.punchline: Her condescending dismissal of his previous work
jokes.explanation: Establishes Kate's snobbery effectively - the 'real play' distinction is perfectly pretentious

jokes.text: That infomercial. For the milk-carton-spout thing. You're the guy who doesn't know how to pour milk.
jokes.setup: Kate suddenly remembering where she's seen Joey
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific and embarrassing context
jokes.explanation: 'The guy who doesn't know how to pour milk' is hilariously specific and deflating after his soap opera boasting

jokes.text: See, I actually can pour milk. But I got you believing that I couldn't. Now, see, that's acting.
jokes.setup: Kate mocking his infomercial appearance
jokes.punchline: Joey's attempt to spin embarrassing infomercial as serious acting
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey logic - turning humiliation into pride with completely sincere conviction

jokes.text: Right. At the end, you choked on a cookie. Yeah, that was real.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming his milk-pouring incompetence was acting
jokes.punchline: Admitting the cookie choking was genuine
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of his acting defense - the casual admission makes it funnier

jokes.text: Okay, so it's probably just the Ross thing.
jokes.setup: Phoebe suggesting Rachel has issues with Ross and her father, Rachel denies father issues
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's quick logical elimination
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe directness - matter-of-fact elimination of possibilities

jokes.text: Well, maybe the crazy fog has lifted and she realizes that life without me sucks.
jokes.setup: Ross wondering why Rachel wants to see him
jokes.punchline: His delusional optimism about their breakup
jokes.explanation: 'Crazy fog' is a good metaphor for Ross's perspective on their relationship issues

jokes.text: It's possible. You are very lovable. I'd miss you if I broke up with you.
jokes.setup: Ross expressing hope about Rachel wanting him back
jokes.punchline: Joey's unexpectedly intimate and awkward support
jokes.explanation: Joey's earnest attempt to be supportive comes out oddly romantic and inappropriate

jokes.text: [IN DEEP VOICE] If I broke up with you, I'd miss you.
jokes.setup: Ross asking Joey to be supportive like a guy
jokes.punchline: Joey's exaggerated masculine voice saying the exact same thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey solution - change the delivery but keep the same inappropriate content

jokes.text: Ross, you got that for free from the museum gift shop. It's still a gift. I got it from the gift shop.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming a mug was a gift to Rachel
jokes.punchline: His insistence that getting it free from work still counts as a gift
jokes.explanation: Ross's desperate logic about what constitutes a gift reveals his pettiness perfectly

jokes.text: You are a petty man. You are a petty, petty... Petty... petty, petty... small... small...
jokes.setup: Rachel calling Ross petty
jokes.punchline: The escalating repetition as Ross presumably struggles with the tiny shirt
jokes.explanation: The repetition building to 'small' while Ross struggles with the small shirt is perfect physical comedy timing

jokes.text: You haven't worn that since you were 15. It doesn't even fit you anymore. Oh, yeah, yeah, yeah.
jokes.setup: Rachel pointing out the shirt doesn't fit Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross's defiant agreement while clearly struggling to put it on
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy gold - Ross's stubborn pride overriding obvious reality

jokes.text: What, like, 'Pete Dakota'? Or 'Mississi-Pete.' Ooh, ooh, I got it: 'Pete Chicago.'
jokes.setup: Suggestion that Pete should buy a state and name it after himself
jokes.punchline: Joey's increasingly ridiculous state name suggestions
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey geographical confusion - 'Mississi-Pete' is the best of his attempts

jokes.text: That's not a state, Joe. Oh, and 'Mississi-Pete' is?
jokes.setup: Chandler pointing out Chicago isn't a state
jokes.punchline: Joey's defensive response about his made-up state name
jokes.explanation: Joey's indignant response shows he thinks his made-up name is more legitimate than Chicago

jokes.text: Okay. You're our age. Okay.
jokes.setup: Pete leaving after their conversation
jokes.punchline: Continued fixation on Pete being their age despite his wealth
jokes.explanation: Running gag about their amazement at his age/success ratio - feels repetitive

jokes.text: Okay, I'm running out of places I can touch him.
jokes.setup: Monica saying goodbye to Pete with minimal physical contact
jokes.punchline: Her admission about the limited contact in their relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the awkwardness of dating someone you're not attracted to - very relatable

jokes.text: Yeah, kind of.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if something's wrong with her for only being attracted to unavailable men
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt, honest confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe response - completely honest when everyone expects reassurance

jokes.text: Tasty.
jokes.setup: Dramatic stage kiss during rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Joey's inappropriate comment on the kiss
jokes.explanation: Typical Joey inappropriate comment - breaks the theatrical moment with crude simplicity

jokes.text: Oh, hey, how about this one? Uh, it says so in the script.
jokes.setup: Kate asking for deeper motivation for character attraction
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal interpretation that the script is reason enough
jokes.explanation: Joey's anti-method approach to acting - why overthink when the script tells you what to do?

jokes.text: It doesn't say that in the script. It does in mine.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming the script calls Kate's character a bitch
jokes.punchline: Implying he wrote that in himself
jokes.explanation: Clever reveal that Joey has been editing his script - shows his pettiness perfectly

jokes.text: Does it bug you? You bug me.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if his cowlick bugs Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler's blunt response that Ross himself is the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan - maximum impact with minimal words

jokes.text: Is there any chance you didn't see that?
jokes.setup: Ross presumably doing something embarrassing at the peephole
jokes.punchline: His hopeful question about witnesses
jokes.explanation: Classic embarrassment recovery attempt - everyone's been there

jokes.text: You can't just stare through the peephole for three hours. You're gonna get peep-eye.
jokes.setup: Ross obsessively watching through the peephole
jokes.punchline: Chandler's made-up medical condition
jokes.explanation: 'Peep-eye' is a clever made-up condition that sounds plausible - good wordplay

jokes.text: I mean, bullets have left guns slower.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how quickly Ross slept with someone after thinking he broke up
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated comparison to bullet speed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant hyperbolic comparison - perfectly captures the speed and creates a vivid, quotable image

jokes.text: And the inventor of the door rests happily in his grave.
jokes.setup: Ross complaining he can't see through the closed door
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sardonic observation about doors working as intended
jokes.explanation: Masterful Chandler line - absurd, philosophical, and perfectly sardonic. The door 'inventor' concept is brilliantly ridiculous

jokes.text: That way, if one of them is missing, she can be like, 'Where's number 27?'
jokes.setup: Explaining Monica's numbered coffee mugs system
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of Monica's organizational method
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica characterization - the numbered system is exactly her type of obsessive organization

jokes.text: Look, you wanna get back at Ross, I am here for you. Really. No, no. I say we get back at him right here on this couch. Right here.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she's using Mark to get back at Ross
jokes.punchline: Mark's eager willingness to be used for revenge sex
jokes.explanation: Mark's enthusiasm for being a rebound is both pathetic and understandable - decent character comedy

jokes.text: I can just go home and get back at him by myself.
jokes.setup: Mark being rejected for revenge sex
jokes.punchline: Implying he'll masturbate as his own form of revenge
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly dark joke for network TV - the implication is both sad and funny

jokes.text: Well, with all these doctors and nurses... I'm gonna say, Midget Rodeo.
jokes.setup: Monica asking where they are in what's clearly a hospital
jokes.punchline: Chandler's completely absurd alternative explanation
jokes.explanation: 'Midget Rodeo' is wonderfully absurd and unexpected - classic Chandler non-sequitur

jokes.text: Yeah. I know I'm no Jon Bon Jovi.
jokes.setup: Pete being told Monica isn't attracted to him
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific celebrity comparison for attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Jon Bon Jovi as the attractiveness standard is both dated and endearing - shows Pete's age

jokes.text: Yeah, stupidly charming, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Monica noting Pete's confidence despite being rejected
jokes.punchline: Pete acknowledging his own charm with more charm
jokes.explanation: Self-aware charm that proves his point - Pete's confidence becomes endearing rather than arrogant

jokes.text: I don't think this town is big enough for the both of us to relax in.
jokes.setup: Ross wearing the tiny shirt and trying to be dramatic
jokes.punchline: Western showdown parody while clearly uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Classic Western parody undermined by his physical discomfort - the shirt makes it funnier

jokes.text: I wasn't doing anything.
jokes.setup: Dramatic Western standoff moment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's complete lack of participation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deflation of Ross's drama - refuses to play along

jokes.text: Ah, Sliced Bread. A wonderful Lady Macbeth.
jokes.setup: Joey's mangled metaphor about Kate thinking she's great since sliced bread
jokes.punchline: Chandler treating 'Sliced Bread' as an actual actress
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that turns Joey's cliché into absurd theater criticism - 'wonderful Lady Macbeth' is perfect

jokes.text: God, I just... I hate her with her 'Oh, I'm so talented' and 'Oh, I'm so pretty,' and 'Oh, I smell so good.'
jokes.setup: Joey listing what he hates about Kate
jokes.punchline: The progression from professional to physical to oddly specific smell complaint
jokes.explanation: 'I smell so good' is wonderfully specific and reveals Joey's been paying close attention

jokes.text: I think somebody has a crush on somebody. Hey, Chandler, can we please try and stay focused on my problem here? You know? I'm talking about you, you big, big freak.
jokes.setup: Chandler suggesting someone has a crush, Joey thinks it's about him
jokes.punchline: Chandler clarifying he means Joey has the crush
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection followed by the gentle 'big freak' insult - Joey's obliviousness is perfect

jokes.text: If you were in school yard... you'd be pulling her pigtails and pushing her down now. Oh, yeah? Then how come I keep thinking about her... in all these sexual scenarios and stuff, huh?
jokes.setup: Chandler's innocent playground metaphor for Joey's behavior
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate leap to sexual thoughts
jokes.explanation: Joey completely missing the metaphor and revealing his actual thoughts - very in character

jokes.text: You guys make me fly. High.
jokes.setup: Emotional stage performance with Kate
jokes.punchline: Joey's awkward attempt to give feedback to other actors
jokes.explanation: Joey trying to sound sophisticated but coming off awkward - the correction to 'high' makes it worse

jokes.text: Why isn't it 'Spiderman'? You know, like Goldman, Silverman? Because it's not his last name. It isn't? No, it's not, like... Like Phil Spiderman.
jokes.setup: Joey questioning Spider-Man's name format
jokes.punchline: The concept of 'Phil Spiderman' as a regular guy
jokes.explanation: 'Phil Spiderman' is brilliantly mundane - Joey's literal thinking creates great absurdity

jokes.text: But there's no gold man. Oh, okay. There should be a Gold Man.
jokes.setup: Discussion of name patterns and actual gold men
jokes.punchline: Joey's decisive conclusion that Gold Man should exist
jokes.explanation: Joey's simple solution to perceived injustice - if there's Goldman, there should be Gold Man

jokes.text: Although I did sit down where there wasn't a chair.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying work went great
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning an embarrassing physical mishap
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup delivered with perfect casual understatement

jokes.text: What about things that are already gold? Ah. His work is done.
jokes.setup: Monica asking about Gold Man's powers on already gold items
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact response about completed work
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical extension of the Gold Man concept - his job satisfaction with pre-existing gold

jokes.text: All right, you yellow-bellied, lily-livered... Draw. Yeah, it's fun.
jokes.setup: Ross trying another Western showdown in the tiny shirt
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic assessment of the 'game'
jokes.explanation: Chandler's deadpan dismissal of Ross's continued Western fantasy while uncomfortable in tiny shirt

tmdb_overview: Rachel goes on her first date after splitting from Ross with Mark. Joey is cast in a play and develops a crush on his costar.
